author: Eddie H
msg:
Is there any other settings for apple user to help keep unity connected? I have the below points 

iOS check list:
1. Do not swipe the app away. Always have in open in the background.
2.  Go to the Settings > General > Background App Refresh and ensure it is enabled for Unity.
3. Try to not enable Low Power save mode as it can interfere. 
4. Enable notifications in both device settings + inside the app.
5. Go to the Settings > Screen Time > add Unity to the list of "‘Always Allowed".

author: R.J.
msg: [Photo] Two of my devices started to get this VPN warning 2 days ago. I don't have any VPN on my devices. Theses two have Sim cards and are connected to my wifi. I have two other devices,  no Sim, that are always connected to my wifi and they don't have any issues.  So my guess is when I leave the house,  and those two devices with  Sim switch to mobile network (T-Mobile),  triggers this warning and disconnects the node. When i restart service again at home it reconnects without any issues.  Anyone else has  started experiencing this?

I have the same issue.  Here is how support responded.

What you can do now:

1. Reboot your router to request a new public IP
2. If unchanged, ask your ISP to assign a different IP/routing path


Best regards,
Unity Nodes Support

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Okay, last thing for me for an hour or so, then I realy need a break. I am not hyping up anything. I fully aknowledge that there are things that are not well, hings that need improvements. The ONLY thing I am saying, is that things are not as bleak as some people try to paint them. We have seen progress, we have seen the potential with S&R and there is no big plot to scam anyone out of their money. Compared to lots of other investments this one is ALREADY giving you a return. The constant whining about the same things over and over again that have been explained and disproven, like the 96$ right from the start and such things is just silly. Complaining that your rewards are lower when your uptime is below 50% is silly. And there are lots of other things. 

Again: THERE ARE THINGS THAT ARE NOT WELL AND NOBODY DISPUTES THAT! But rehashing the same thing over and over again and overlooking whats actually good/promising is ALSO a problem.

And with that I will go spend some time with my actual kids. For those at least I need to take responsibility and can have fun with them 😂

msg: And for the record my little farm is 7 phones. I have no farms.

author: Abdus
msg: Just going to share an opinion. Telemetry is a passive task with “massive” limitations. I think it may have best to prioritise more passive tasks. There seems to be mixed opinions about S&R. I am not saying other passive tasks would have been more profitable. I am just speculating there may have been less frustration. Telemetry info was very useful to point out how the system was being gamed. We know other tasks will be suitable for current “screenless phone” set-ups and this has been confirmed. Could it be waiting for these tasks to be released would have caused more unrest? Commercial/operational/technical delays? Yes, S&R was released because of higher rewards. Not saying this is untrue. What is the full picture? The evidence would seem to point that other tasks were not ready. And yes the enigmatic CLI and SMS dilemma. We are still waiting on volume for those. Yes confirmed volume inbound. But reality check is also necessary. I am just reasoning things out here. Food for thought for everyone. No need to come charging and bashing every word I am saying. Just think about it. I am walking a fine line trying to balance for and against in my head here. There is a button that has a warning “Do not release the kraken.“ I think this is a fair summary.

author: Rocky NOW pool
msg:
I have close to 500 leases filled now, so the dynamic has shifted a bit. When I was running around 10 personal phones on WiFi, it was easier to be patient with delays or changing timelines.

With a few hundred individuals relying on me as their ULO, there’s a greater sense of responsibility. When updates are shared with us and we pass them along, and those timelines don’t hold, it can create some friction on our end since we’re the ones communicating directly with everyone.

There is only ONE thing Unity needs to do and that is to get rewards up to $10 a month. $5UNO/$5ULO. That’s it. It is insanely easy to onboard users at those prices of 16 cents a day. 

Finally give us transparency. Be honest with us. Stop Bull Sh**ing  us with partnerships and recalibration. Treat us UNOs as teams members. 
JMhO

author: Pilla
msg:
The ambiguity is in my opinion, deliberate.

Imagine if in November/December they told us tasks are 4-5 months out, after hyping up the sale as a network that’s ready to launch after a years testing WITH a backlog of work.

It’s why we’ve been given 

“You’ll definitely have multiple tasks in January”

“You’ll have tasks during February”

“You’ll have tasks late February, early march”

“You will have tasks in march”

“Tasks in 7-14 days”

“Tasks in 7-10 days”

“Tasks in days, not weeks”

With no tasks delivered to date (apart from telemetry majority are not happy with)

I think we’ll still be asking for January tasks in may!!
